commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Feishu reply dispatch so oversized final streaming replies bypass CardKit streaming, discard any active preview, and use existing chunked message/static-card delivery.

PR surface: Source +5, Tests +77. Total +82 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection. Current main routes final Feishu streaming replies through CardKit before chunking, and the PR demonstrates the fixed branch with targeted dispatcher tests and local live-output proof.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed from this review; the remaining action is normal PR review, checks, and author/maintainer readiness.

target.title: fix(feishu): preserve long streaming replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep oversized Feishu final replies off the streaming-card path when they exceed the configured text chunk limit, including auto-mode markdown/code-block/table replies.
- Discard any active streaming preview before delivering oversized final replies through the existing chunked message/static-card send path.
- Add focused coverage for oversized plain and markdown final replies plus partial-preview cleanup.

Fixes #88631.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts` (66 tests)
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main`
- One-shot local Feishu-compatible HTTP proof harness using the real dispatcher, real chunker, real `sendMessageFeishu`/`sendStructuredCardFeishu`, and the real Lark SDK `Client.im.message.create` request path.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Feishu streaming no longer routes oversized plain or auto markdown/table/code-block final replies into streaming CardKit content that can exceed CardKit element limits.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout with a one-shot Feishu-compatible HTTP endpoint. The proof exercised `extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts`, `extensions/feishu/src/send.ts`, `extensions/feishu/src/client.ts`, `extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.ts`, and the installed `@larksuiteoapi/node-sdk` client request path. This is real OpenClaw/Lark SDK code-path proof, not a mocked unit test and not a live Feishu tenant.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the one-shot Node proof harness after the predicate fix, then reran the focused Vitest file and formatting/diff checks listed above.

Evidence after fix: Plain oversized final with no partial preview produced 3 post chunks (`10,10,3`) and `cardkit_calls=0`. Plain oversized final after an active partial preview logged streaming start, `DELETE /open-apis/im/v1/messages/om_3`, then the same three post chunks. Oversized markdown/code-block final with no partial preview produced `interactive_create_calls=8` static card sends and `cardkit_calls=0`.

Observed result after fix: Oversized plain final text is delivered as chunked Feishu post messages, while oversized markdown/code-block final text is delivered as chunked static interactive cards. Neither path closes the final answer through a streaming CardKit card; existing partial previews are explicitly discarded before final chunked delivery.

What was not tested: A live Feishu tenant/CardKit API call was not run; this checkout has no configured Feishu/Lark app credentials or target, and repo search found no Feishu secret-backed live lane.
target.author: monkeyleet
